title: Reorder Point Calculator
metaTitle: Reorder Point Calculator — When to Reorder Inventory (Formula + Examples)
metaDescription: Calculate your inventory reorder point, safety stock, and days of supply. Prevent Amazon stockouts and overstocking with the reorder point formula for ecommerce sellers.

inputs.label: Average Daily Sales (units)
inputs.helpText: Average units sold per day. Use 30-day average from Seller Central or your store analytics.

inputs.label: Lead Time (days)
inputs.helpText: Days from placing a purchase order to inventory arriving at your warehouse or FBA. Include production + transit + receiving time.

inputs.label: Safety Stock (days of cover)
inputs.helpText: Extra buffer inventory measured in days of supply. 14 days is typical for stable products; 30+ days for high-demand or long lead-time items.

inputs.label: Current Inventory (units)
inputs.helpText: Your current on-hand inventory (total across all locations)

inputs.label: Unit Cost (COGS)
inputs.helpText: Cost per unit — used to calculate the dollar value of your reorder quantity

inputs.label: Target Reorder Quantity (days of stock)
inputs.helpText: How many days of stock to order each time. 60 days is typical for FBA sellers balancing storage fees and stockout risk.

formulaCitation: CSCMP — Supply Chain Management Terms and Glossary (2023); Investopedia — Reorder Point Formula (2024)
formulaSource: Reorder Point = (Average Daily Sales × Lead Time) + Safety Stock; Safety Stock = Average Daily Sales × Safety Stock Days; Days of Supply = Current Inventory / Average Daily Sales; Reorder Qty = Average Daily Sales × Target Stock Days
